description: Cross-language parity fixture for hub/recursive/bridge.{py,js}. Both bridges are bespoke (they share no code with the _shared stream_utils), so this fixture guards the observable parity claim in hub/README.md ('both produce identical AgentProc output'). It exercises the pure, side-effect-free helpers that determine the bridge's observable behaviour: provider_args/providerArgs and _global_args/globalArgs (which RECURSIVE_* + AGENT_STREAMING env vars map to which CLI flags, in what order), extract_session_dir/extractSessionDir (parsing the 'session: recording to <dir>' stderr line), and _last_assistant_text/lastAssistantText (reading the last assistant turn from transcript.jsonl). The full NDJSON event classification (handleLine inside main) is not covered here — it is nested in main() and not yet refactored to an importable helper; that remains future parity work. Both bridge.py (importable via the __main__ guard) and bridge.js (importable via the require.main guard) are driven by the same cases, so a divergence shows up as one test failing against this shared expectation. For arg_cases, the test clears all RECURSIVE_* and AGENT_STREAMING vars, sets exactly the ones in `env`, then calls the builder.
